1.11a: Address: New Business Department, Foundation, 3 Arlington Square, Downshire Way, Bracknell, RG12 1WA
1.11b: Witnessing of documents requires a physical third party present
Video conferencing is not acceptable for witnessing documents
Video conferencing for Independent Legal Advice requires a Distance Independent Legal Advice Certificate
1.14: Firms may act if a separate partner or fee earner of equal standing acts for the lender
Must be no conflict of interest
Must comply with professional rules and guidelines
1.15: Firms may act if a separate partner or fee earner of equal standing acts for the lender
Must be no conflict of interest
Must comply with professional rules and guidelines
3.1.3: Notification of seller's conveyancer details is not required
Conveyancer must satisfy themselves of the seller's conveyancer identity
Must advise if acting for both seller and purchaser
3.1.4: Transactions where the seller does not have legal representation are unacceptable
3.1.5: Accepted identity docs (List A): EU Member State National ID, Foreign National ID with indefinite leave to remain, Northern Ireland Electoral Office ID card
Accepted identity docs (List B): Old style full UK driving licence, state benefit entitlement evidence, tax credit or pension evidence
Bank or Credit Card statements from regulated UK firms are accepted, but internet copies are prohibited
3.2.3: Notification of seller's conveyancer details is not required
Must advise if acting for both seller and purchaser
3.2.4: Transactions where the seller does not have legal representation are unacceptable
4.5a: Contact New Business Department via 1.11a for re-inspections
4.5b: Submit Certificate of Title electronically via the solicitor panel management portal
4.1: Valuation report provided only if a physical interior inspection was performed
Valuation reports are for lender use only and cannot be relied upon by the borrower
4.3: Report incorrect valuation assumptions to the New Business Department (1.11a)
5.1.1: Borrowers must have owned the property for 6 months for remortgages
6-month rule exceptions: bridging loan replacement, cash purchase with evidenced funds, or inherited properties (requires written confirmation)
Sub-sales and back-to-back transactions are not acceptable
Freehold/superior title grants of lease to a buyer by a seller who just acquired it are treated as sub-sales and are prohibited
Transactions involving assignable, transferable, or novation contracts are prohibited
Irrevocable powers of attorney for intervening sellers are prohibited
5.2.1: Sub-sales and back-to-back transactions are prohibited
Assignable contracts and irrevocable powers of attorney for intervening sellers are prohibited
Refer cases where the seller is not the owner/registered proprietor to the lender (1.11a)
5.4.4: Do not send environmental reports unless they identify matters affecting security
5.4.5: Personal searches accepted if an unqualified Certificate of Title is provided
Search agents must follow the Search Code (COPSO) and be monitored by PCCB
5.4.6: No Search indemnity insurance accepted for remortgages if sum insured equals open market value and title is clear
No Search insurance prohibited for remortgages of: HMOs, MUBs, MPOTs, and Mixed-use properties
No Search indemnity insurance accepted for purchases if sum insured equals market value and title is clear
No Search insurance prohibited for purchases of: HMOs, MUBs, MPOTs, and Mixed-use properties
5.5.3a: HMOs and Multi Units must have all required consents and licences
Indemnity insurance for alterations over 10 years old may be considered case-by-case
5.5.3b: Do not send planning permission documents unless requested; retain on file
5.5.3c: Do not send building regulation consents unless requested; retain on file
5.5.3d: Do not send certificates of lawful use unless requested; retain on file
5.5.4: Report restrictions affecting value or marketability to 1.11a; indemnity may be considered
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds accepted if arrangements ensure good/marketable title
Refer flying freeholds to the lender if they exceed 20% of the total floor area
5.7.1b: Freehold flats are not accepted, except for Tyneside flats with mutual covenants
5.7.1c: Report freehold flats or flying freeholds to 1.11a
5.8.1: Security for buildings with up to 4 flats accepted if first legal charge is held over leasehold and freehold/superior titles
A trust deed must ensure freehold and leasehold titles transfer simultaneously upon sale
Report if the borrower owns any other flat in the building
5.8.5: Requires first legal charge over both the leasehold interest and the freehold reversion of the other flat
Lease must require simultaneous transfer of freehold share with lease sale
5.13.1: Gifted deposits from immediate family do not need reporting if not repayable
Accepted family: parents, grandparents, siblings, children, spouse, civil partner, aunts, uncles
Second charges or Deeds of Trust to protect gifts are prohibited
Bankruptcy searches required against gift donors
Gifts from outside the UK must be referred to 1.11a
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance for absent landlords accepted on a case-by-case basis; must be bespoke policies
5.14.17a: Do not send documentation unless specified in 14.2.2; retain on file
5.14.17b: Report non-qualifying leaseholders and remediation costs under Building Safety Act 2022 immediately
Report remediation work requirements before exchange of contracts
Completion prohibited until written instructions received following Building Safety reports
5.14.17c: Building Safety Act instructions in 5.14.17b apply to remortgages
5.14.1: New Build houses: minimum 250 years unexpired at start
New Build flats: minimum 125 years unexpired at start
Non-new build: minimum 50 years remaining at end of mortgage term
5.14.9: Ground rent increases linked to RPI/CPI are acceptable
Ground rent doubling or monetary increases every 20+ years are acceptable if reasonable
Ground rent doubling in under 20 years is unacceptable
Ground rent increases linked to property value are unacceptable
Starting ground rent cap: 0.1% of market value (refer to lender if 0.1% to 0.2%)
If ground rent creates an AST risk, lease must be varied to <£250 (£1000 in London) or indemnity insurance must be used
5.14.10: Report service charge increases exceeding £1000 per annum
Report planned works including list, timescale, total cost, and borrower contribution
5.14.11: Indemnity for unsatisfactory lease terms accepted case-by-case; must be bespoke if necessary
5.14.12: Do not send ground rent/service charge receipts; ensure no arrears at completion
5.14.13: Send receipted copy of notice or evidence of service with title documents after completion
5.15.2b: Do not send share certificates; borrower to retain, conveyancer to keep copy on file
5.15.2c: Send a copy of the signed blank stock transfer form to the lender; retain original on file
5.15.2d: Do not send Memorandum and Articles of Association
5.17.5a: Do not send Power of Attorney; retain on file
5.17.5b: Do not send statutory declaration of non-revocation; retain on file
5.19.1: Affordable housing, shared equity, and shared ownership schemes are not accepted
5.20.1: Report solar PV panel leases that do not meet UK Finance minimum requirements to 1.11a
5.20.3: Lender requires unconditional right to break solar PV lease upon possession
Removal costs and damage repair must be the responsibility of the solar PV provider
5.20.4: Existing Green Deal Plans must be disclosed to 1.11a
6.1.3: Report if borrower is not taking up offer to 1.11a
6.4.4: Report all incentives using the UK Finance Disclosure form
Notify lender if any part of the deposit for a non-new build is paid by the seller
Finders fees are not acceptable and must be reported
Chancel repair premiums and defective title insurance are not considered reportable incentives
6.5.1: Vacant possession required for residential properties at completion
6.6.2: Properties must be let under approved AST within 3 months of completion
6.6.3: Do not send tenancy agreements; retain on file
6.7.1: Accepted warranties: ABC+, Advantage (ADHCI), Aedis, RIBA Architects Certificate, Ark, Build Assure, BLP (no reservations), Build Zone, CADIS, Castle 10, CGICE schemes, RICS/RIBA Certificate, Checkmate, Global, ICW, LABC, NHBC, One Guarantee, Premier (no reservations), Protek, Q Build Assure, Elite Insurance (specific admin), Zurich Municipal
All other schemes require referral to 1.11a
6.7.4: If no warranty, work must be monitored by a qualified professional
Professional Consultant’s Certificate (PCC) must follow UK Finance format
PCC for flats must cover common parts and structure
6.7.6: Send copy of Professional Consultant's Certificate to lender; borrower retains original
6.8.1: Report absence of road/sewer agreements and bonds to 1.11a
6.9.1: Verify that servient and dominant tenements have not merged in common ownership
6.10.2: Retentions are released to the borrower after completion
7.3: Consent forms required for all occupants aged 17 and over
BTL properties require AST/Standard Occupation Contract ensuring lender priority
9.1: Do not send indemnity policy; retain copy on file, borrower retains original
9.2: Minimum indemnity limit: 110% of valuation or purchase price (whichever is higher)
10.2b: Minimum notice for funds: 5 working days
10.7: Return delayed funds immediately to the originating account
10.10: Funds can be held for the day of completion only; return immediately if completion fails
10.11: Interest charged at the offer rate from date of advance until funds re-credited
12.3.1: No retentions or phased funds release offered
14.1.5: Register charge via certified copy at Land Registry
Limited liability companies/partnerships must register at Companies House (Form MR01) within 21 days
Retain original deeds (Legal Charge, TID, Guarantees, Consents, ILA confirmation, Incentives, MR01 certificate, Stat Decs) on file
14.2.1: Send title deeds and documents via email to customerservices@foundationlending.co.uk
14.2.2: Post-completion requirements: Complete ‘eDeed Packet’ Checklist, email pdf copies certified as true by Solicitor/FCILEX/Licensed Conveyancer
16.1.1: Title document contact: Customer Services Department, 3 Arlington Square, RG12 1WA or DX 122530 Bracknell (2)
16.3.7b: Standard attestation: Signed as a Deed by Paratus AMC Limited by its Attorneys... in the presence of...
17.2.1b: Discharge is sent directly to the Land Registry
